title: UGC Strategy: Turning Your Customers into Your Best Creators
author: Chloe Wang
excerpt: User-generated content outperforms brand content by 4x on average. Here's how to build a UGC engine that feeds your organic and paid channels — without spending a fortune.
body:
## Why UGC Wins

We've tested this across every client account we manage: user-generated content consistently outperforms brand-created content. On average, UGC gets **4x the engagement** and **2.5x the click-through rate** compared to polished brand posts.

Why? Because it looks real. And real is what people trust.

## How to Build a UGC Engine

### Step 1: Make Sharing Effortless

The number one reason customers don't post about your brand is friction. Make it easy: include social handles on packaging, create a branded hashtag, and tell people exactly what to post. "Tag us in your morning routine" is better than "Share your experience."

### Step 2: Incentivise Without Cheapening

Don't pay for UGC — earn it. The best incentives are recognition (reposting their content), access (early drops, behind-the-scenes), and community (making them feel like insiders). Cash incentives attract content farms, not genuine advocates.

### Step 3: Curate Ruthlessly

Not all UGC is good UGC. Build a simple scoring system: Does it show the product clearly? Is the quality acceptable for reposting? Does it match your brand tone? Only reshare content that meets all three criteria.

### Step 4: Repurpose for Paid

The real power of UGC is in paid ads. We take top-performing organic UGC and run it as Spark Ads on TikTok or partnership ads on Meta. The results are consistently 2-3x better than traditional ad creative.

## The Takeaway

You don't need a massive content budget. You need a system that turns happy customers into willing creators — and a strategy that puts their content to work across every channel.
